name: Beef Stuffed Shells
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the ground beef, onion, and garlic in a skillet over medium heat until browned. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. Cook the pasta shells in boiling water for about 15 minutes, then drain.
3. In a large bowl, combine the cooked meat mixture with shredded mozzarella cheese, bread crumbs, egg, and seasonings (salt, pepper, parsley, oregano). Mix well.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
5. Spread 1/4 of the spaghetti sauce into a baking dish or lasagna pan.
6. Stuff each pasta shell with the meat mixture and place in the baking dish on top of the sauce.
7. Pour the remaining sauce over the stuffed shells, sprinkle with grated parmesan cheese.
8. Cover with foil and bake for 20-25 minutes.
9. For freezing: Cook the shells first, let them cool, then freeze in a freezer-safe container or cover with foil before assembling. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and cook at 350°F (175°C) until heated through. Alternatively, freeze the stuffed shells before cooking, then thaw and cook as directed. You may need to add extra sauce when reheating from frozen.
Note: I have also used more mozzarella cheese since we like things cheesy!
